provenance.source_corpus: Seedling size and woody competition most important predictors of growth following free-to-grow assessments in four boreal forest plantations

source_spans.text: We found that seedling size accounted for most of the variation in height and volume growth of jack pine (Pinus banksiana Lamb.) and black spruce ( Picea mariana [Mill.] BSP) plantations.
We found that seedling size accounted for most of the variation in height and volume growth of jack pine (Pinus banksiana Lamb.) and black spruce ( Picea mariana [Mill.] BSP) plantations.

In the plantation initiation phase (years 2 to 6), the presence of herbaceous competitors generally reduced conifer growth but in the first part of the stem-exclusion phase (years 7 to 12) increased their growth.

In the plantation initiation phase (years 2 to 6), the presence of herbaceous competitors generally reduced conifer growth but in the first part of the stem-exclusion phase (years 7 to 12) increased their growth.

In the plantation initiation phase (years 2 to 6), the presence of herbaceous competitors generally reduced conifer growth but in the first part of the stem-exclusion phase (years 7 to 12) increased their growth.
In all four boreal plantations in this study, woody competitors reduced conifer growth in both the initiation and stem-exclusion phases.
